name: Musayyab ibn Najaba
title: Commander of Tawwabin
shortDescription: A brave commander of Kufa and companion of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who fought alongside Sulayman in the Tawwabin uprising and was martyred.
fullStory:
Musayyab ibn Najaba al-Fazari was a brave and loyal companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) who had shown great heroism in the battles during his time. Like Sulayman ibn Surad, he was among those who wrote to Imam Hussain (AS) but failed to assist him on Ashura.

The fire of regret burned Musayyab like the other Shias of Kufa. Along with Sulayman ibn Surad, they formed the core of the Tawwabin uprising. In his impassioned speeches, he called on the Kufans to make amends for their shortcomings.

Musayyab was Sulayman's right-hand man in the Tawwabin uprising. In the battle of 'Ayn al-Warda' against the army of Sham, Musayyab commanded a section of the army.

When Sulayman ibn Surad was martyred, the flag of command fell to Musayyab. With indescribable bravery, he struck the heart of the enemy army and, after killing dozens, achieved his desire: martyrdom in the path of avenging Hussain (AS).
quoteArabic: قَدِ ابْتَلانَا اللَّهُ بِعُمُرٍ طَوِيلٍ تَعَرَّضْنا فِيهِ لِلْفِتَنِ... فَما عُذْرُنا عِنْدَ رَبِّنا فِي خِذْلانِ ابْنِ بِنْتِ نَبِيِّنا...
quote: (His speech among the Tawwabin:) We were deceived... What is our excuse before God for abandoning the son of our Prophet's daughter, except to kill his murderers?
